task: Restore live Google Tag Manager and GA4 collection after the merged fail-closed CSP gate blocked every deployed ResearchOps page.
selectionNote: The operating-model selector included GOV.UK and Mural from generic content/tag wording; no GOV.UK or Mural implementation is changed.
decision: Revert the fail-closed merge commit with mainline parent to restore the already-tested GTM and GA4 CSP allowlist, its route-state tests, and the corresponding governance evidence without rewriting history.
evidence: A live crawl found GTM markup on 65 of 66 static routes; /pages/synthesize/ is an intentional legacy redirect exception.
All 66 live responses blocked www.googletagmanager.com in CSP after PR #494 merged.
The user explicitly directed restoration of live tagging.
residualRisk: The restored harm and gap registers record that non-essential analytics remains subject to privacy, consent, data-flow and opt-out escalation.
